summary: Tested Shelfsort Library safety report feature end-to-end. Backend: 7/7 pytest cases pass (GET /api/account/safety shape + user isolation + auth gate, POST /api/account/safety/rescan persists last_rescan_at, recent_infected empty for clean library, /api/health regression with antivirus.available=true under .checks.antivirus). Frontend: /account/safety page renders fully — three stat cards with counts + percentages, safety-rescan-btn triggers rescan + safety-last-rescan label updates with timestamp/summary, safety-recent-infected correctly hidden for clean library. /account page now exposes data-testid='safety-report-link' (href=/account/safety) next to backup-restore-link. ClamAV daemon healthy in pod (av_available=true). Tester library shows 4 books, all unscanned in this preview pod (the live rescan reported scanned=0 because the user's STORAGE_DIR has no on-disk files — files live in emergent_object_storage and rescan only walks the local cache, as documented in the route's docstring; main agent's curl on its run scanned 2 because the cache was warm then).

minor.issue: By design scans only files present in STORAGE_DIR/<user_id> on local disk; with emergent_object_storage as the active storage backend the user_dir is often empty after a pod recycle, so scanned=0 and unscanned books stay unscanned. Not a bug per the docstring, but worth surfacing as a known UX gap if the team expects rescan to also touch cloud-stored files.

action_items: (Optional, scope-call) Decide whether 'Rescan my library' should also fetch from cloud storage instead of only walking the local STORAGE_DIR cache — current behaviour leaves cloud-resident files perpetually 'Awaiting first scan' for users whose pod hasn't downloaded their files yet.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/account_safety.py — clean separation from admin antivirus surface, auth via get_current_user, ObjectId stripped from av_quarantine + user_rescans projections, 503 path correctly surfaces antivirus.is_available()=false.
Rescan caps at 500 files per call to prevent worker pinning — good. asyncio.to_thread(antivirus.scan_path, fp) keeps the event loop healthy during ClamAV calls.
Per-file book lookup uses {book_id, user_id} compound filter on db.books update_one — correctly scoped (a malicious book_id collision across users can't flip another user's av_status).

AccountSafety.jsx — proper loading/error/success states, percentages computed safely (total>0 guard), recent_infected list conditionally rendered, rescan button disabled when rescanning OR av_available=false, toast error falls back to err.response.data.detail.
STORAGE_DIR is read from env at import-time (good — matches every other storage-aware module). Local copy comment in source explains the circular-import dodge clearly.
Minor nit: routes/account_safety.py uses `import os as _os` solely for one env lookup — could be hoisted to a module-level `_STORAGE_DIR` constant for symmetry with utils.config, but harmless.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — all required data-testids render, rescan flow + last-rescan label verified, account-page link verified

seed_data_creation: None. user_rescans collection now has one document for user_a18dde45958d (created by the rescan test) — idempotent upsert so re-runs reuse the same row.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend WatchFiles reloader is very sensitive to any edit under /app/backend/tests/ — wait ~15s after touching a test file or restart backend before running pytest, otherwise calls time out for ~2min while uvicorn reloads. Tests use TEST_BASE_URL=http://localhost:8001 fallback when REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL is set to the preview URL but the SSL handshake stalls; both work. Frontend /account requires waiting for AuthContext to hydrate before goto('/account') — pattern: login via context.request.post → goto('/') → wait 3s → skip tour → goto('/account') → wait 3-4s. The tester library currently has 4 books and 0 files in STORAGE_DIR/<user_id>, so rescan returns scanned=0; this is expected with emergent_object_storage active.
